papers.title: Gradient-Based Learning Applied to Document Recognition
papers.key_finding: End-to-end trained convolutional networks beat hand-engineered features for document recognition.
papers.abstract: Reviews gradient-based learning for pattern recognition and shows that convolutional neural networks trained by backpropagation outperform hand-designed feature pipelines on handwritten digit and document recognition.

tags_provenance.quote: Convolutional neural networks are specifically designed to deal with the variability of 2-D shapes.
Better pattern recognition systems can be built by relying more on automatic learning and less on hand-designed heuristics.

papers.title: A Fast Learning Algorithm for Deep Belief Nets
papers.key_finding: Greedy layer-wise unsupervised pretraining makes very deep networks trainable.
papers.abstract: Introduces a fast, greedy layer-by-layer algorithm for learning deep belief networks one layer at a time, reviving the practical training of deep architectures.

tags_provenance.quote: We derive a fast, greedy algorithm that can learn deep, directed belief networks one layer at a time.

papers.title: ImageNet Classification with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
papers.key_finding: A large GPU-trained CNN with dropout and ReLU roughly halved ImageNet error.
papers.abstract: Trains a large deep convolutional network (AlexNet) on ImageNet using ReLU units, GPU training, and dropout regularization, dramatically lowering top-5 error and igniting the deep-learning era for vision.

tags_provenance.quote: Recently-developed regularization method called dropout proved very effective at reducing overfitting.
On the test data we achieved top-5 error rates considerably better than the previous state of the art.

papers.title: Efficient Estimation of Word Representations in Vector Space
papers.key_finding: Shallow log-linear models learn high-quality word vectors at scale, cheaply.
papers.abstract: Proposes the continuous bag-of-words and skip-gram architectures (word2vec) for learning distributed word representations efficiently from very large corpora.

tags_provenance.quote: We propose two novel model architectures for computing continuous vector representations of words from very large data sets.

papers.title: Sequence to Sequence Learning with Neural Networks
papers.key_finding: An LSTM encoder–decoder maps whole sequences to sequences, enabling neural machine translation.
papers.abstract: Presents a general end-to-end sequence-to-sequence approach using multilayered LSTMs to encode an input sequence to a fixed vector and decode the target sequence, achieving strong machine-translation results.

tags_provenance.quote: We use a multilayered Long Short-Term Memory to map the input sequence to a vector of a fixed dimensionality.

papers.title: Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate
papers.key_finding: Letting the decoder attend over source positions removes seq2seq's fixed-vector bottleneck.
papers.abstract: Introduces an attention mechanism that lets a neural translation model softly search the source sentence for relevant positions when generating each target word, improving translation of long sentences.

tags_provenance.quote: The model (soft-)searches for a set of positions in a source sentence where the most relevant information is concentrated.

papers.notes: Review: confirm whether to add a see_also link to word2vec; double-check author affiliations before marking reviewed.

papers.title: Attention Is All You Need
papers.key_finding: Replacing recurrence with pure self-attention sets new translation SOTA and parallelizes training.
papers.abstract: Proposes the Transformer, a sequence-transduction architecture based solely on self-attention without recurrence or convolution, achieving state-of-the-art translation quality with far more parallelizable training.

tags_provenance.quote: We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely.
Our model achieves a new state of the art while being more parallelizable and requiring significantly less time to train.

papers.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
papers.key_finding: Masked bidirectional pretraining of a Transformer yields a general-purpose language encoder.
papers.abstract: Introduces BERT, which pretrains deep bidirectional Transformer representations using a masked-language-modeling objective, then fine-tunes to state-of-the-art results across many language-understanding tasks.

tags_provenance.quote: BERT is designed to pretrain deep bidirectional representations by jointly conditioning on both left and right context.

papers.notes: Drafted by LLM — verify the methods tags and the builds_on link before reviewing.

papers.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
papers.key_finding: Scaling a Transformer LM to 175B parameters enables few-shot learning with no gradient updates.
papers.abstract: Shows that scaling an autoregressive Transformer language model (GPT-3, 175B parameters) lets it perform many tasks from a few in-context examples without any gradient-based fine-tuning.

tags_provenance.quote: GPT-3 is applied without any gradient updates or fine-tuning, with tasks specified purely via text interaction with the model.

papers.notes: Drafted by LLM — author list truncated to a representative subset; confirm modality.

papers.title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale
papers.key_finding: A pure Transformer over image patches matches CNNs at scale, bridging vision and language.
papers.abstract: Applies a standard Transformer directly to sequences of image patches (Vision Transformer), showing that with large-scale pretraining it matches or exceeds convolutional networks on image classification.

tags_provenance.quote: A pure transformer applied directly to sequences of image patches can perform very well on image classification tasks.
